En: You can now see your statistics on your JOURNAL page -- to the right of the task description.
Zh: 现在你可以在日志界面看到自己的统计信息——就在任务描述右侧。

En: I'm sure I've seen worse.
Zh: “我确定自己见过更糟糕的。”

En: Oh yes. Coal City, Le Royaume, the Burnt Out Quarter...
Zh: “哦，没错。煤城，王国，燃尽区……”

En: All of them worse than here.
Zh: 都比这里要糟糕的多。

En: *Alpha male officers who are proud of their numbers* often do -- it's meant.
Zh: *为自己的数字感到骄傲的大男子主义警官*经常这么做——是这个意思。

En: Son, here is where we score your life performance. You better hope it's good.
Zh: 小子，这里就是我们给你的生活表现打分的地方。你最好希望分数不错。

En: That's right. A killer of humans.
Zh: 没错。人类的杀手。

En: *Confirmed kills*? That sounds pretty evil. A *drink* would soften that feeling.
Zh: *确认击杀数？*听起来好像挺邪恶的。如果来*一杯酒*，就能抚慰这种感受。

En: It's not a problem for him to state it, however.
Zh: 不过，陈述这件事对他来说不是问题。

En: It's meant in earnest. Please don't mistake it for a jab.
Zh: 这是认真的。请不要把它误会成批评。

En: Neither am I. Are we done here?
Zh: “我也不知道。完事了吗？”

En: Wait, *eighteen years* I've done this?
Zh: “等等，我已经做这个*18年*了吗？”

En: That's what it says. I might've guessed even longer based on your age... what did you do all those blissful years of your youth?
Zh: “上面就是这么写的。以你的年纪来看，我猜可能会更久……你在无忧无虑的年轻时代都做了些什么？

En: Not bad.
Zh: 还不赖。

En: Not bad for what? You don't even know what it means yet.
Zh: 什么还不赖？你都不知道是什么意思。

En: He's sincerely glad you're not a scary predator. Not to say relieved you're competent.
Zh: 你不是一个可怕的掠夺者，这一点让他由衷地感到高兴。现在还不能放心地说你能够胜任。

En: A rat brazenly darts past you and disappears amongst the stopped lorries. In the distance -- a child somewhere shrieks, a woman reprimands her in a voice no quieter than the child's cry...
Zh: 一只老鼠厚脸皮地从你身边窜过，消失在停着的货車中间。远方——有个孩子在尖叫，一个女人的训斥声比小孩的哭声还要大……

En: Ah, Martinaise, he smells the air and says.
Zh: “啊，马丁内斯，”他深吸一口气，开口说到。

En: In the middle of a broken plaza, in a cone of light barely visible in the daytime, two men, one slim, the other sturdy. They are on the city stage, but only one of them knows his lines...
Zh: 在破败的广场中央，圆锥形的光线映照出两个男人，一个瘦小，另一个壮实。他们站在城市的舞台中央，但是只有一个人知道自己的台词……

En: I know you've killed some people in the line of duty...
Zh: “我知道，你在执行任务的时候杀过人。”

En: As you hold your ledger's clip under the headlamp, a shimmering iridescent pattern appears. One you've definitely seen before...
Zh: 当你把手册拿到前灯下，一个闪烁的虹光图案出现在你眼前。你之前肯定见过……

En: Around the borders of the watermark are dozens, no, *hundreds* of micro-perforations.
Zh: 水印的边缘是数十个，不对，是“数百个”微穿孔。

En: It's not unlike the pattern from the racist lorryman's boots. A stylized map of Revachol.
Zh: 不像是种族主义货車司机的靴子图案。一份程式化的瑞瓦肖地图。

En: As you hold your ledger's clip under the headlamp, an iridescent hologram appears: a street grid and the veins of a great river. A familiar sensation washes over you...
Zh: 当你把手册拿到前灯下，一个闪烁的全息图出现在你眼前。一个街道网，还有大河的脉络。一种熟悉的感觉贯穿你的全身……

En: There she is: Revachol West. There's a note of pride in the lieutenant's voice.
Zh: “有了：瑞瓦肖西部。”警督的声音里透露出一丝骄傲的味道。

En: Look at the shimmering street grid.
Zh: 看着闪闪发亮的街道网。

En: Look at the perforations.
Zh: 看着穿孔。

En: Okay, let's go. [Put the ledger away.]
Zh: “好吧，我们走。”[把手册放到一边。]

En: There are many of them. And they are divided into three separate rows.
Zh: 穿孔有很多，被分成了三行。

En: Tally up the different rows.
Zh: 把不同的行数加起来。

En: The first row has 18 dots.
Zh: 第一行有18个点。

En: What about the next one?
Zh: 下一行呢？

En: The next is the longest. It runs all the way around the border and then some.
Zh: 下一行是最长的。沿着边缘一直延伸。

En: There are so many it's hard to count. More than 150, at least. Maybe even 200.
Zh: 太多了，很难数清。至少超过150，甚至是200。

En: What about the last row?
Zh: 那最后一排呢？

En: The last row has three perforations.
Zh: 最后一排有3个穿孔。

En: Three, that's it?
Zh: 3个，就这么多？

En: That's it.
Zh: 就这些。

En: Hey, Kim, what do all these holes mean? (Point to the dots on the watermark.)
Zh: “我说，金，你觉得这些孔是什么意思？”（指着水印上的孔洞。）

En: Those are *perforations*. They represent your record as an officer of the RCM. They're your statistics, as it were. I should have guessed you'd keep a record, officers often do. Let's take a look...
Zh: “那是*穿孔*。代表你作为RCM警官的记录。可以说是你的统计数据。我早该猜到应该有你的记录，警官都会记录的。让我来看看……”

En: The first row represents your years of service. *Eighteen* years? Okay -- not bad at all. What did you do before you volunteered?
Zh: “第一行代表你的服务年限。*18*年？好吧——挺不错的。你自愿加入之前是做什么的？”

En: Got drunk like a megastar?
Zh: “像个超级巨星一样天天买醉？”

En: I walked the land telling whores and liars of the End to come. There are 9,855 days remaining.
Zh: “我在这片土地四处游走，告诉妓女和骗子们末日即将来临。现在还剩下9855天。”

En: Do you really think I have any idea?
Zh: “你真觉得我知道吗？”

En: Yes, that *does* seem quite likely. Your youth coincided with some heady days for Revachol. But let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “是的，看起来*确实*如此。你的青春跟瑞瓦肖的某些好日子是重叠的。不过我们还是继续吧，好吗？”

En: Fair point... Let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “说得好……我们继续吧，好吗？”

En: Cool. I'm glad you joined us. Not a lot of money in doomcrying... Let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “挺酷的。很高兴你加入了我们。在末日里哭泣可不值几个钱……我们继续吧，好吗？”

En: I was a hobo and I lived under a boat.
Zh: “我曾经是个流浪汉，住在一艘船下面。”

En: You know, the RCM is a volunteer's organization. We would have no qualms with vagrants joining our ranks were they qualified... but let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “你知道的，RCM是一个志愿者组织。如果流浪汉够资格加入我们的行列，我们并不会因此感到不安……我们继续工作吧，好吗？”

En: No shame in working a regular job, detective. Sometimes after a long day of doing *this*, I even envy it... Let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “做一份普通的工作没有什么好羞愧的，警探。有时候，在整整干了一天*这种活*之后，我甚至有点妒忌他们……我们继续吧，好吗？”

En: Probably a some boring office job. Same as everyone else.
Zh: “大概是某种无聊的办公室工作吧。跟其他人一样。”

En: I feel like I just went around apologizing all the time.
Zh: “感觉我一直在到处道歉。”

En: Maybe you were a diplomat? Or in PR? Doesn't matter, I suppose... Let's move on, shall we?
Zh: “也许你是个外交官？或者是公共关系行业的？不过我猜，也不重要了……我们继续吧，好吗？”

En: This next row -- the one that wraps all the way around -- is your number of closed cases. *Closed* is good. It means finished. You've got, let's see...
Zh: “下一行——就是绕了一整圈的这个——代表你的结案数。好意义上的*结案*。代表破获的案件……你这里有……我看看……”

En: Wow, more than 200!
Zh: “哇哦，超过200个了！”

En: Two-hundred sixteen, to be exact.
Zh: “准确的说，是216个。”

En: Is that a lot?
Zh: “很多吗？”

En: I would have thought there'd be more.
Zh: “我以为还会有更多呢。”

En: It's *quite* a lot, even for someone who's been on the force for nearly two decades. Usually clearing more than 10 cases a year puts you in the 90th percentile of *all* RCM officers...
Zh: “已经*相当*多了，就算对于一个在警队待了快20年人来说，也很多了。一般来说，一年破获10多起案件就能超过RCM里90%的警员……”

En: The lieutenant nods with evident respect. "It's quite a lot, even for someone who's been on the force for nearly two decades. Usually clearing more than 10 cases a year puts you in the 90th percentile of *all* RCM officers..."
Zh: 警督充满敬意地点点头。“已经*相当*多了，就算对于一个在警队待了快20年人来说，也很多了。一般来说，一年破获10多起案件就能超过RCM里90%的警员……”

En: See, Kim? I *told* you I was a superstar cop!
Zh: “看见没，金？我*告诉*过你，我是明星警探！”

En: So you're saying I used to be a *supercop*?
Zh: “所以你是说，我曾经是个*超级警探*？”

En: I used to be good. That's some solace I guess. What's the last number?
Zh: “曾经挺好的。我猜这也是一种安慰吧。那最后一个数字是什么？”

En: I don't think I can ever *re-become* this person... What's the last number?
Zh: “我不觉得自己会*重新变回*那个人……最后一行数字是什么？”

En: Doesn't that tingle in your index finger give it away?
Zh: 你食指上的刺痛感没了吗？

En: Right. Those are your confirmed kills. You've got precisely *three* perforations there.
Zh: “没错。那是你确认的杀人数。这里刚好有*3个*穿孔。”

En: So I'm a killer...
Zh: “所以我是个杀手了……”

En: I was expecting a higher number, honestly.
Zh: “说实话，我还以为这个数字会更高。”

En: That's not... too many.
Zh: “那好像……不是很多。”

En: Is now a good time to pour one out for the fallen?
Zh: “现在是不是为倒下的那些人干杯的好时候？”

En: Definitely not.
Zh: “绝对不是。”

En: Call it what you want. You were a valuable member of your precinct. Now, let's look at the last row...
Zh: “随你怎么称呼吧。对于你的分局来说，你是很有用的一员。现在来看看最后一行……”

En: Have you ever killed anyone, Kim?
Zh: “你杀过人吗，金？”

En: How do you handle the strain?
Zh: “你是怎么处理压力的？”

En: Thanks for this. (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢你。”（结束）

En: The lieutenant nods.
Zh: 警督点点头。

En: Everyone has their own method of coping, some more effective, or self-destructive, than others... He gives you a meaningful look.
Zh: “每个警察都用自己的方式，有些效率更高，或者更有自毁倾向，相比其他人来说……”他意味深长的看了你一眼。

En: Yes, he says, declining to elaborate.
Zh: “是的，”他回答到，不愿意详说。

En: (Let's look at something else.)
Zh: （我们来看点别的吧。）

En: Right. The meaning of the perforations remains opaque to you.
Zh: 没错。你还是不明白穿孔的含义。

En: As you look at them you feel your index finger reflexively contract three times.
Zh: 当你看着它们，突然感觉到你的食指反射性地收缩了三次。

En: Seems like something I've done before.
Zh: 好像跟我之前做过的事有关。

En: A memory, an echo in the recesses of your amygdala.
Zh: 一段记忆，你杏仁核深处的回音。

En: Must be a muscle spasm.
Zh: 一定是肌肉痉挛。

En: And something you'll do again, before this is all over.
Zh: 而且是你还会继续做的事，直到这一切结束。

En: You know the answer already. The tingle in your index finger gave it away.
Zh: 你已经知道答案了。食指上的刺痛感也随之消失。

En: Those are my confirmed kills. Three of them.
Zh: “那些是我杀过的人数量。三个。”

En: That's right, the lieutenant says, gauging your reaction. "How did you know?"
Zh: “没错，”警督一边回答，一边猜测你的反应。“你怎么知道的？”

En: Because I felt a twitch in my index finger.
Zh: “因为我感到食指上的刺痛。”

En: Because I feel like a killer.
Zh: “因为我觉得自己是个杀手。”

En: Just a lucky guess.
Zh: “只是碰巧猜中了。”

En: Ah, right. Sometimes the brain's able to preserve certain responses even when conscious memory has been... let's say... *misplaced*...
Zh: “啊，是的。有时候大脑能够保存特定的反应，即使是外显记忆已经……这么说吧……*错置*的情况下……”

En: The rectangular watermark is overlaid with the logo of the RCM, and yet the major arteries of Revachol are all recognizable. They shimmer in the Kineema's headlights.
Zh: 长方形的水印被RCM的标志覆盖着，但瑞瓦肖的要道还是全都清晰可见。它们在锐影的前灯下闪闪发光。

En: Wait... look around you.
Zh: 等等……看看你周围。

En: Where are we on this? (Point to the halogen map.)
Zh: “我们在哪个位置？”（指着卤素灯地图。）

En: You catch a faint glimmer from a broken beer bottle. In the distance -- sounds. Two men engaged in a drunken argument, followed by the closing of some distant window.
Zh: 你在一只破碎的啤酒瓶上注意到一丝微弱的闪光。远处——传来了声音。两个醉醺醺的男人在争吵，接着是远处窗户关闭的声音。

En: In the middle of a broken plaza, illuminated in a cone of light, two men, one slim, the other sturdy. They are on the city stage, but only one of them knows his lines...
Zh: 在破败的广场中央，圆锥形的光线映照出两个男人，一个瘦小，另一个壮实。他们站在城市的舞台中央，但是只有一个人知道自己的台词……

En: Ah, Martinaise at night, he smells the air and says.
Zh: “啊，马丁内斯的夜晚，”他吸了一口气，说到。

En: Let me see. He takes the ledger for a moment and inspects it.
Zh: “我来看看。”他拿起手册，仔细看了一会儿。

En: Right here, he says, his finger near the top of the map, on a segment of coast jutting out into the great ocean.
Zh: “就在这，”他的手指落在地图顶部附近，海岸上一段伸入大洋的地方。

En: Count them individually.
Zh: 挨个挨个数。

En: You count 216 perforations. Considering that nice, large number, a wave of pride washes over you, though you can't say why, precisely.
Zh: 你数了216个穿孔。一想到这个漂亮又庞大的数字，你感觉全身被一股自豪感环绕，虽然你还不清楚到底为什么会这样。

En: For an RCM officer -- especially Precinct 41, which is in the Jamrock Quarter -- it's rather... tame. I mean that in a good way.
Zh: “对于一个RCM警官来说——特别是位于加姆洛克的41分局——其实……挺平淡的。我指的是好的方面。”

En: What's it feel like to kill a man, Mr. McCoy? a young woman asks the man across the desk from her...
Zh: “杀人是什么感觉，麦考伊先生？”一个年轻女子向路过桌前的男人发问。

En: Honestly, babe, says John McCoy, crossing his ankles over said desk, "I don't feel anything anymore. It's just like brushing my teeth -- I do it once or twice a week and don't really think about it." There's no trace of guilt in his voice.
Zh: “宝贝，说实话，”约翰·麦考伊把双脚交叉放在桌子上说到，“我已经没什么感觉了。就像在刷牙——每周一次或者两次，所以真的没想过。”他的声音里没有半分内疚的意思。

En: There are certain officers who treat their kills like some kind of ghoulish game. If they do happen to *solve* a case it's usually by accident. It's obvious the lieutenant doesn't think very highly of these officers...
Zh: “局里有些警官会把杀人当成是某种残忍的游戏。如果他们碰巧*解决*了一起案件，通常都是意外。”警督对那些警官的评价显然不高……

En: But it seems as though you are, or at least *were*, one of the good ones. So we have that to be thankful for.
Zh: “不过好像你是，或者至少*曾经*是个好警察。所以应该对此心存感激。”

En: Are you *sure* I shouldn't pour one out for those fallen boys?
Zh: “你*确定*我不应该为那些死去的男孩敬一杯酒吗？”

En: Maybe I should pour one out for the boys? The fallen?
Zh: “也许我该给那些男孩们敬一杯酒？死去的那些？”

En: The lieutenant sighs.
Zh: 警督叹了口气。

En: If you want to pour out the booze you've been carrying around *on duty*, I'm certainly not going to stop you...
Zh: “如果你想在*值勤*的时候用身上带着的酒敬上一杯，我肯定不会阻止你……”

En: Actually, I'm just gonna hold onto this.
Zh: “其实，我只想抓着它而已。”

En: (Pour one out on the ground -- ceremonially.)
Zh: （把酒洒在地上——非常仪式感的那种。）

En: A dark pool of cheap wine appears at your feet. It shimmers in the lights of the Kineema.
Zh: 你的脚下出现一滩黑色的廉价酒。在锐影灯光的照射下闪闪发光。

En: Honourably done. You have given up some of your own blood -- for theirs.
Zh: 值得尊敬。你放弃了自己的部分血液——都是为了他们。

En: As you will...
Zh: “如你所愿……”

En: The Gloaming comes for all, but it came first for you.
Zh: “黄昏终将降临在所有人身上，但它首先找到的是你。”

En: I'm sorry for killing you.
Zh: “对不起，我杀了你。”

En: Here's to you, honoured dead.
Zh: “敬你们一杯，死去的人。”

En: I will make it okay with the power of rock and roll.
Zh: “我要用摇滚的力量让一切好起来。”

En: I'm not really sure what you're supposed to say at these things.
Zh: “我真的不知道该说些什么好。”

En: Very noble of you. Are we done here?
Zh: “你的行为很高尚。完事了吗？”

En: Personally, I find it helps to keep up a few hobbies.
Zh: “就我自己来说，我觉得保持一些爱好是有所帮助的。”

En: Like TipTop racing?
Zh: “比如大师赛？”

En: Like what?
Zh: “比如说？”

En: *Par exemple*. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: “*例如*。”警督点点头。

En: Oh, this and that. Let's not get into it now.
Zh: “哦，各种各样的东西吧。现在还是不要太深入展开的好。”

En: Maybe I should find a hobby?
Zh: “也许我也应该找点爱好？”

En: Hobbies are lame.
Zh: “爱好太无聊了。”

En: Suit yourself...
Zh: “随你便吧……”

En: Why not gardening? You've already got the gloves... He points to your yellow gardening gloves.
Zh: “为什么不试试园艺呢？你都已经有手套了……”他指着你的黄色园艺手套。

En: Why not gardening? You've already got the yellow gloves...
Zh: “为什么不试试园艺呢？你都已经有黄手套了……”

En: Why not historical reenactment? You've already got that antique carabineer gear... He points to your carabineer swag.
Zh: “为什么不试试历史重现呢？你都已经弄到那套古老的卡宾枪手装备了……”他指向你的卡宾枪手战利品。

En: Why not historical reenactment? You've already got that antique carabineer gear...
Zh: “为什么不试试历史重现呢？你都已经弄到那套古老的卡宾枪手装备了……”

En: Why not take up a sport? You've already got that FALN gear... He gestures toward your performance gear.
Zh: “为什么不试试运动呢？你已经有FALN的装备了……”他朝着你的功能套装做了个手势。

En: Why not take up a sport? You've already got that FALN gear...
Zh: “为什么不试试运动呢？你已经有FALN的装备了……”

En: Why not historical reenactment? You've already got that turn of the century revolutionary hat... He points to your hat.
Zh: “为什么不试试历史重现呢？你都已经弄到那顶世纪之交的大革命帽子了……”他指向你的帽子。

En: Why not historical reenactment? You've already got that turn of the century revolutionary hat...
Zh: “为什么不试试历史重现呢？你都已经弄到那顶世纪之交的大革命帽子了……”

En: Anything's better than annihilating yourself with drugs and alcohol...
Zh: “什么都比用毒品和药物摧毁自己的好……”

En: A dark pool of moonshine-spiked borscht appears at your feet. The chunks of beet shimmer in the lights of the Kineema.
Zh: 你的脚下出现一滩黑色的烈性罗宋汤。大块的甜菜在锐影灯光的照射下闪闪发光。

En: A dark pool of pale-aged vodka appears at your feet. It shimmers in the lights of the Kineema.
Zh: 你的脚下出现一滩黑色的陈年伏特加，在锐影灯光的照射下闪闪发光。

En: A dark pool of cheap pilsner appears at your feet. It shimmers in the lights of the Kineema.
Zh: 你的脚下出现一滩黑色的廉价比尔森啤酒，在锐影灯光的照射下闪闪发光。

En: It seems as though you don't have anything to pour out. Too bad for the boys.
Zh: 你没有可能拿来敬酒的东西。对于那帮男孩来说，实在是太糟糕了。

En: Seems nice.
Zh: “好像不错。”

En: Seems like a shithole.
Zh: “很像个粪坑。”

En: No -- it does *not*, the lieutenant says without optimism.
Zh: “不——*算不上*，”警督好不乐观地说道。

En: It has its problems...
Zh: “它有自己的问题……”

En: Right. I'll go turn off the lights... He presses a remote control on the key.
Zh: “是的。我要把灯关了……”他按下钥匙上的远程控制按钮。

En: There's pride in there. A trained driver, *knows* his ride.
Zh: 这里透露出一种自豪。一个训练有素的老司机，*很懂*他的座驾。

En: It's a bit *girly* right now. Fit it with some proper off-road components.
Zh: 现在感觉有点*娘*了。还是装一些合适的越野组件吧。

En: Lingo it up.
Zh: 说行话。

En: Saying these words brings him immense joy.
Zh: 这些话给他带来了巨大的快乐。

En: Wow... he's not happy you called him a binoclard. He'll get over it.
Zh: 哇哦……他好像不是很喜欢你叫他四眼仔。不过他自己会克服的。

En: ... and means *no*.
Zh: ……而且这就意味着“不要”。

En: I might not know much, but I *do* know I like motor carriages -- must be a subconscious thing.
Zh: “我也许知道的不多，但是我*知道*自己喜欢汽車——肯定是潜意识里的东西吧。”

En: Right, the lieutenant marks dryly. "Speed isn't for everyone."
Zh: “好吧，”警督干巴巴地回应到。“速度可不是每个人都吃得消的。”

En: What?! Speed most definitely *is* for... Oh... he means velocity. Can't really get properly high on that so... Nevermind.
Zh: 什么？！速度肯定*就是*……哦……他说的是物理速度。那个快了确实也谈不上享受……无所谓了。

En: What?! Speed isn't for...?! Oh... he means velocity. Yeah, that's definitely an acquired taste.
Zh: 什么？！速度难道不是……：！哦……他说的是物理速度。是啊，那绝对是一种嗜好。

En: I'm not either. Don't know why I said that.
Zh: “我也不明白。不知道我为什么会这么说。”

En: Well, the lieutenant says, frowning ever so slightly, "maybe don't just say every little thing in your head out loud. It confuses people."
Zh: “好吧，”警督微微皱起眉头，说到：“也许不要把脑子里出现的东西都大声说出来。可能会让别人感到困惑的。”

En: The sole purpose of 'pornography' is to stimulate one's visual sense to evoke sexual arousal -- the same is true with the modifications you're proposing.
Zh: “色情文学”的唯一目的就是刺激人的视觉，来唤起性兴奋——你提出的修改建议也是如此。

En: Alright, I guess it did get a bit porny there.
Zh: “好吧，我猜它确实变得有些色情了。”

En: I'm sincerely relieved I don't have to try and convince you of that. He glances at his vehicle. "Back to work?"
Zh: “不必试图说服你，我还真是松了一口气。”他瞥了一眼自己的車。“继续工作吧？”

En: USE BIGGER WORDS!!!
Zh: 再多说些大话！！！

En: Trust me, it's cool.
Zh: “相信我，一定会很酷的。”

En: No one in the history of convictions has been more sure of *anything*.
Zh: 在定罪的历史上，从未有人对*任何事*如此肯定过。

En: ... the wires go *bweeeeeeeeee* and then *neeeeeeeow*? (Loudly imitate the sounds.)
Zh: “……所以电线是会先*哔哔哔哔哔哔哔*然后再*咪咪咪咪咪咪咪咪咪咪*？”（大声地模仿那个声音。）

En: This is your chance to impress the lieutenant!
Zh: 打动警督的机会来了！

En: Basically, yes -- although it's mostly about frequency range, not the wires making *bwee-*sounds. Still -- I'm impressed.
Zh: “基本上来说，是的——虽然主要跟频率范围有关，而不是电线发出的*哔*声。不过——你确实让我刮目相看。”

En: What a binoclard!
Zh: 真是个四眼仔！

En: Whatever you do, don't make fun of the lieutenant for explaining this to you.
Zh: 不论你做什么，都不要因为警督跟你解释这件事而取笑他。

En: The human lie-detector.
Zh: 人形测谎器。

En: Wait, hundred-and-thirty what?
Kilowatts, the lieutenant replies laconically.
Zh: “千瓦，”警督简洁的回答到。

En: Oh, I thought it would make more.
Zh: “哦，我还以为会更高呢。”

En: Believe me, the lieutenant replies -- sounding a little defensive -- "it's plenty enough. Kineema feels right at home on the raised motor tract. Hard to find something that could overtake it."
Zh: “相信我，”警督回答到——听起来有些防御性的味道——这已经很足够了。锐影在上升高速公路上感觉就像回自己家一样。很难找到可以超过它的了。”

En: He is not enthused about the idea.
Zh: 他对这个想法不是很兴奋。

En: He liked the idea.
Zh: 他喜欢这个想法。

En: Motor carriages don't interest me. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 我对汽車不感兴趣。[完成思维。]

En: (Shrug.) "A motor carriage. One of many."
Zh: （耸肩。）“一辆汽車。芸芸之中的一个。”

En: Harmel-Rioux 'Supersonique' -- a racing motorbike for the road -- could probably do it.
Zh: 哈默尔-里乌的“超音速”——公路摩托赛車——也许能做到。

En: No. (Nod sadly.) "I wish I did."
Zh: “不。”（悲伤地点点头。）“我希望如此。”

En: Okay. The lieutenant nods too. "That's confusing. Can we get back to business now?"
Zh: “好吧。”警督也跟着点了点偷。“这就有点困惑了。好了，现在我们可以继续回去工作了吗？”

En: Wait, why am I even thinking about this? Wasn't I supposed to...
Zh: 等等，我为什么会想起这个啊？难道我不是应该……

En: No time for this, gotta run. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 没时间耽误了，得走了。[完成思维。]

En: ... do something important? Something murder-related? There's always *something* important. Doesn't mean you can't take a moment to admire this piece of machinery.
Zh: ……做点重要的事？跟凶杀案有关的？总是会有*重要*的事。但这不代表你不能稍微休息一下，好好欣赏一下这台机器。

En: Actually motor carriages don't interest me. [Finish thought.]
Zh: 其实我对汽車没什么兴趣。[完成思维。]

En: Even at a standstill the uni-body Coupris Kineema looks sleek and dynamic. The cabin is tilted frontward to give it a more aggressive *hunched* look.
Zh: 就算停在原地，一体成型的库普瑞斯锐影看起来还是时髦又充满活力。前倾的乘坐舱为它*紧凑*的外观增添了几分闯劲。

En: That machine really puts the 'loco' back in 'locomotion'! (Point to the vehicle.) "Very cool."
Zh: “那台机器真是把‘运动’又带回‘旅行’中了！”（指向汽車。）“真是太酷了。”

En: I don't like your machine, lieutenant. Looks impractical.
Zh: “我不喜欢你的車，警督。看起来很不切实际。”

En: Mhmh... the lieutenant huffs. "No point in stopping then."
Zh: “嗯……”警督有些生气。“那就没必要停下来了。”

En: Agreed, let's keep moving. [Finish thought.]
Zh: “同意，我们走吧。”[完成思维。]

En: I'd still like to take a close look.
Zh: “我还想再仔细看看。”

En: Sure, go ahead.
Zh: “当然可以，去吧。”

En: Mhmh. The lieutenant smiles ever so slightly. "You want to take a closer look?"
Zh: “嗯。”警督微微一笑。“你想再靠近点看吗？”

En: This is a Coupris Kineema, the Coupris MotorCorp's follow-up to their highly successful workhorse, Coupris 40, and the answer to the LUM's racing-bred 'Fevre' series.
Zh: 这是一台库普瑞斯锐影，库普瑞斯汽車公司集大成之作——库普瑞斯40的后续产品，针对LUM竞品‘热梦’系列的回答。

En: With its air-cooled, rear-mounted twelve cylinder compression ignition engine driving the rear wheels through a four-speed manual gearbox, the Kineema is able to reach 100 kilometres per hour in 13.5 seconds. And go on to a top speed of 180 kilometres an hour.
Zh: 拥有空气冷却，后置十二缸压缩点火发动机的它能通过四速手动变速箱驱动后轮，锐影能够在13.5秒内达到100公里/小时的速度，最高速度能达到每小时180公里。

En: The high centre of balance is offset by a large battery bank mounted at the bottom of the cabin, feeding all the auxiliary systems and making the Kineema effectively a mobile power plant.
Zh: 高重心被乘坐舱底部的后置大电池所抵消，而且还能供给所有附属系统，这就让锐影变成了一个高效的移动发电厂。

En: Due to a quite steep price tag it is very unusual to see one in police livery.
Zh: 由于价格过于高昂，所以警察系统不是很常见。

En: What in there made the infernal whining sound that woke me?
Zh: “是什么东西发出这么烦人的声音，把我都吵醒了？”

En: A fine machine... (Run your hand over the smooth metal surface.)
Zh: “一台好机器……”（用手抚摸光滑的金属表面。）

En: Okay, let's move. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我们走。”[离开。]

En: You mean the coil noise?
Zh: “你说的是线圈的噪音？”

En: When variable current passes through wires that make up the coils on the electrical systems on this machine -- it causes vibration in the wires and the cores of the coils.
Zh: “每当可变电流穿越电线的时候，就能为机器的电气系统的线圈提供补偿——造成电线和线圈核心的振动。”

En: When the frequency of the current in the coils falls within the audible range  the resulting vibration creates the *whiny sound* you mentioned.
Zh: “当线圈内的电流频率落入可听范围，产生的振动就会发出你口中的那个*烦人的声音*。”

En: You are such a binoclard, Kim.
Zh: “你真的太符合四眼仔的身份了，金。”

En: Wow... how do you know all that?
Zh: “哇哦……你是怎么知道这些的？”

En: Mhmh, that's exactly what I thought.
Zh: “嗯，我也是这么想的。”

En: The resulting vibration creates the whining sound, that's right, the lieutenant finishes your sentence. "Very impressive."
Zh: “产生的振动发出了烦人的声音，是这样没错，”警督接完了你的话。“挺厉害的嘛。”

En: There's respect in that curious look. Definitely.
Zh: 那股好奇的神情里带着一丝尊敬的味道。绝对是这样。

En: I have pretty much maintained my vehicles by myself ever since one was assigned to me. You inevitably pick up some knowledge on the way.
Zh: “車分到我手里的时候，很多时候都是我自己做的保养。所以过程中难免会学会一些新知识。”

En: Of course... His gaze wanders over your face.
Zh: “当然了……”他凝视着你的脸。

En: Yes. There's gentleness in the lieutenant's voice as his eyes run over the vehicle's contours. "An extraordinary machine."
Zh: “是的。”警督的目光扫过汽車的轮廓，声音也变得亲切柔和起来。“确实是台非凡的机器。”

En: What's it packing there? (Point to the engine.)
Zh: “里面装的是什么？”（指向引擎。）

En: You need to *slam* it, Kim. Make it more imposing.
Zh: “你得*用力砰的一下*关上它，金。这样更让人印象深刻。”

En: Sorry, I'm not following you?
Zh: “抱歉，我没明白你的意思？”

En: With a winch and mud tyres we could take it off the beaten path, Kim.
Zh: “来个绞車，再来点肮脏的轮胎，我们就能让它不走寻常路了，金。”

En: We are not going to though, because this is clearly a *sports* motor carriage. Sounds like he has a strong concept of what this machine is.
Zh: “我们不会这么做的，因为它显然是一台*跑車*。”听起来他对这台机器的概念非常清晰。

En: Drop the ride *two hundo mill*, get the camber to frosty-frosty minuses...
Zh: “把引擎下降到*200转*，让外倾角会有结霜的缺点……”

En: No, that's... *porno*-tuning.
Zh: “不，那就叫……*色情*改装了（过度改装）。”

En: It needs a *MASSIVE* aero-kit and *GARGANTUAN* roof-mounted...
Zh: “它需要*巨大的*空气动力组件，还有*庞大的*顶部附件……”

En: Porno. That's short for pornography.
Zh: “色情。那是色情文学的简称。”

En: So? What's wrong with pornography?
Zh: “所以？色情文学怎么了？”

En: That's a negative on the porno. Thank you, though.
Zh: “拒绝色情改装。不过还是谢谢你了。”

En: Hundred-and-thirty.
(Let out a whistle.) "Momma's serving some serious macaroni."
Zh: （吹个口哨。）“看来还真不是闹着玩的。”

En: You lost me there. He is looking at you, perplexed.
Zh: “你把我弄糊涂了。”他困惑地看着你。

En: Hundred-and-thirty kilowatts is a lot of power, Kim.
Zh: “130千瓦的功率相当大了，金。”

En: The Kineema *is* fast. Feels right at home on the raised motor tract. Hard to find something that could overtake it.
Zh: “锐影*的确*很快。在上升高速公路感觉就像回到家一样。很难找到可以超越它的东西。”

En: That's what... (Rub your chin.) "... a seven-litre V12?"
Zh: “那就是……”（摩擦你的下巴。）“……7公升V12吗？”

En: Ever thought about switching to helium headlights?
Zh: “有没有想过换成氦气灯？”

En: Actually, I have a pair at home -- just haven't gotten around to fitting them yet. I need to lay some wiring for the ballasts first...
Zh: “事实上，我家里有一对——只是还没来得及把它们装好。我要先给镇流器铺上电线……”

En: If we ever get this case solved, maybe we can do it together?
Zh: “如果我们破了这个案子，也许可以一起弄？”

En: Maybe, he replies with an apologetic smile and nods. "Yes, definitely maybe."
Zh: “也许吧，”他露出充满的歉意的笑容，点头回应道。“是啊，确实可以。”

En: Sounds like a hassle, but good luck.
Zh: “听起来好像很麻烦，不过还是祝你好运了。”

En: Thanks. The lieutenant smiles. "Are we done here?"
Zh: “谢谢。”警督笑了。“这里的事都处理完了吗？”

En: Seven-point-two. Supercharged. The lieutenant is trying to suppress a smug smile. Unsuccessfully.
Zh: “7.2。超动力。”警督努力想要抑制住得意的笑容。但没有成功。

En: It must be an advancement of the KR18GU engine on the older *Forty* models, right?
Zh: “一定是旧*40*型号用的KR18GU引擎的升级版，对吧？”

En: Yeah, you know it? The lieutenant seems surprised.
Zh: “是啊，你知道的吗？”警督看上来有些吃惊。

En: Just the basics. Cast-iron block, swirl-chamber-injection, dual-chains driving overhead camshafts, two valves per cylinder, hydraulic valve lash adjustment...
Zh: “只知道些基础的。铸铁缸体，涡流室喷射，双链式架空传动凸轮轴，双阀门汽缸，液压阀间隙调整……”

En: Uh... yes, that's right. He stares at you in surprise. "I am very impressed that you know these things."
Zh: “啊……是的，没错。”他吃惊地看着你。“我挺吃惊的，你居然知道这些事。”

En: Oh... The lieutenant rubs the back of his head. "Yes, let's definitely *think* about it."
Zh: “哦……”警督摩挲着后脑勺。“是的，我们当然应该*考虑*一下。”

En: I am not a *binoclard*, I am a driver... and a police lieutenant.
Zh: “我不是*四眼仔*，我是个司机……还是个警督。”

En: Poor choice of words. Lieutenant Kitsuragi obviously doesn't want to be a *binoclard*.
Zh: 出言不慎。曷城警督显然不想当*四眼仔*。

En: Binoclard... binoclard... he repeats to himself, eyes fixed on the ground.
Zh: “四眼仔……四眼仔……”他喃喃自语，眼睛紧盯着地面。

En: Whatever high-school bullying memories you woke in your colleague, he doesn't enjoy them.
Zh: 不论你的同事回忆起中学遭受的何种霸凌，他都很不喜欢。

En: I'm sorry. I don't even know what it means. It just popped into my head.
Zh: “对不起。我甚至都不知道它是什么意思。这个词就是突然在我脑子里出现了。”

En: Speak up, *four-eyes*, I can't hear you!
Zh: “大声点，*四眼*，我听不见你在说什么！”

En: I'm perfectly proud of my glasses. The lieutenant adjusts his spectacles. "I need them for seeing. Anyway..."
Zh: “我为自己的眼镜感到很骄傲。”警督调整了一下他的眼镜。“我需要它们才能看见。无所谓了……”

En: Maybe don't blurt out everything that *pops into your head*. Just some friendly advice. Anyway...
Zh: “也许不要让*突然出现在你脑子里*的东西脱口而出的好。只是个友好的建议而已。无所谓了……”

En: In the morning light the white-on-blue police livery on the motor carriage cannot but catch your eye.
Zh: 清晨的阳光中，汽車上那套蓝白色的警察制服吸引了你的注意。

En: Won't it roll over in the first sharp corner?
Zh: 它不会在第一个急转弯处翻車吗？

En: Turn your attention to the motor carriage itself.
Zh: 把注意力转移到汽車上。

En: This tech-talk is really rubbing me the right way here.
Zh: 这个技术话题真是说到我心坎上了。

En: Someone has waxed it recently.
Zh: 有人最近刚上过蜡。

En: Don't leave fingerprints on the bodywork, I just had it polished.
Zh: “别把指纹留在車体上，我才刚抛光过。”

En: Okay, enough about the details.
Zh: “好吧，细节问题已经差不多了。”

En: Anything else?
Zh: “还有别的事吗？”

En: Hang on, you're telling me you remember all these little mechanical details, but you have trouble remembering your name?
Zh: “等一下，你说自己记得这些小的机械细节，但是却想不起自己的名字？”

En: It's strange, I know. I don't even like motor carriages that much.
Zh: “这很奇怪，我知道。而且我甚至都不是很喜欢汽車。”

En: Strange... but plausible, I guess. He gives you a quick side-glance and turns to go. "I guess you're a bit of a... torque dork too."
Zh: “奇怪……不过我猜也挺合理的。”他飞快地撇了你一眼，然后转身打算离开。“所以我猜你也是个……扭矩呆子吧。”

En: He seems to like the idea.
Zh: 他好像很喜欢这个想法。

En: A motor carriage in white-on-blue police livery.
Zh: 一辆蓝白相间的警車。

En: Droplets of rain fall on the white-on-blue police livery of the motor carriage.
Zh: 雨滴坠落在蓝白相间的警車上。

En: Snow covers the white-on-blue police livery of the motor carriage. The white colours nearly meld together.
Zh: 雪花覆盖在蓝白相间的警車上。两种白色几乎要融合在一起了。

En: The surrounding blaze of the electric lights makes the white-on-blue police livery of the motor carriage seem beautifully ominous.
Zh: 周围电灯发出的光芒让这台蓝白相间的警車变得光彩照人。

En: The afternoon sun shimmers off the white-on-blue police livery of the motor carriage.
Zh: 午后的太阳把蓝白相间的警車映照的闪闪发光。

En: Come on, you know how it works... put it together.
Zh: 来吧，你知道怎么回事……把它组装起来。

En: It causes vibration in the wires and as the frequency of the current happens to fall within the audible range
Zh: “当电流频率正好落入可听范围内时，就会导致电线振动。”

En: You mean when variable current passes through the coils on the electrical systems and the varying magnetic fields around them...
Zh: “你是说当可变电流穿越电气系统的线圈，还有周围不同的磁场时……”

En: He stares at you, intrigued.
Zh: 他好奇地盯着你。

En: What is 'coil noise'?
Zh: “‘线圈噪音’是什么？”

En: I reckon that's a seven-litre V12 there.
Zh: 我估计那个应该是7升V12。

En: An advancement of the KR18GU engine of the Coupris 40, to be precise.
Zh: 准确的说，是库普瑞斯40型KR18GU引擎的升级版。

En: Man, that's got to be a major advancement over the KR18GU engine on the old Coupris 40.
Zh: 天啦，相对于旧库普瑞斯40型的KR18GU引擎来说，这不是升级，是跳级了吧。

En: It's nice and all, but why so modest? Put some zing into it. Flare it up. Slam it down!
Zh: 一切都挺好的，但为什么要这么谦虚呢？给它注入一点活力。把它点燃。然后砰的一声用力摔下去！

En: It's what?
Zh: “那是什么意思？”

En: Pornograpy is something completely different, Kim.
Zh: “色情文学是另一种完全不同的东西，金。”

En: Wait, what about the spinners then, huh?
Zh: “等等，那轮圈呢？

En: Those spinners were elegant, sleek... He pauses, looking for words. "Radiant, even. What you're suggesting is *porno*."
Zh: “那些轮圈很优雅，时髦……”他暂停了一下，搜索着脑中的词汇。“甚至可以说光芒四射。但是你暗示的是*色情*。”

En: Helium headlights would improve the range and quality of the visual field a lot.
Zh: 氦前灯可以大大提升视野的范围和质量。

En: You want to help? He glances at you and smiles. "Thank you for the offer. That might be fun. Let's do the case first though, alright?"
Zh: “你想帮忙？”他瞥了你一眼，笑了笑。“谢谢你的好意。应该会挺有意思的。不过我们还是先调查案子吧？”
